message: So my 8-year-old opened our 'emergency kit' last weekend and asked, 'Why do we have a flashlight in here if the power goes out?' Totally fair point. I realized I'd *made* the kit but never actually walked my kids through what's in it or why. Fast forward to Sunday afternoon—we did a little family tour of our go-bag, our water stash, and went over a super basic 'if we have to leave quick' plan. My daughter immediately started adding her favorite stuffed animal to the bag ('for morale,' she said, and honestly? she's not wrong). Turns out kids are way more likely to stay calm in an actual pinch if they've been part of the planning, not just the stuff showing up out of nowhere. Plus, they remember things better than I do anyway. Win-win. Have you done a 'kit walkthrough' with your family, or is it one of those things sitting in the closet waiting for someday?
